nodes.name: build_de_transition_kernel
nodes.description: Creates a pure Differential Evolution transition kernel from the provided target log-density oracle.

inputs.name: target_log_kernel
inputs.constraints: Stateless log-density oracle; no persistent state mutation.

outputs.name: de_kernel
outputs.constraints: Pure transition function; any stochastic state (e.g., RNG/PRNGKey) must be explicit input/output.

nodes.conceptual_summary: oracle_conditioned_differential_perturbation_kernel_builder. Constructs a higher-order stochastic operator by binding a stateless log-score oracle into a Differential-Evolution-style transition rule. The resulting operator proposes new points by combining existing state vectors with scaled pairwise difference vectors, then uses oracle score differences to probabilistically select the next state. This converts a static compatibility evaluator into a pure, composable Markov transition mechanism with explicit randomness flow.. Applications: Parameter inference for nonlinear biochemical reaction-network models using only simulator-based log-fit or likelihood surrogates, Inverse reconstruction of subsurface physical fields in geophysical imaging from noisy forward-model mismatch scores, Calibration of multibody robotic system parameters (friction, compliance, offsets) from trajectory residual log-scores, Tuning high-dimensional climate or fluid-simulation parameter sets against observational consistency objectives
